close_reason: Style: to_string() vs to_owned() — equivalent, no functional difference
description:
src/lib.rs:141 'SecretVersion::Pinned(v.to_string())' where v is &str (from parsed.param('version')). The /review-rusty rule on this: '.to_string() on a &str literal — use .to_owned() or String::from'. v here is not a literal but a &str borrow; .to_owned() is the correct converter (more direct than going through Display::to_string).

src/lib.rs:180 'Some((project.to_string(), secret.to_string()))' — same pattern. Both are &str -> String conversions where the String is the desired target type, no Display formatting is intended.

Suggested rewrite:
  SecretVersion::Pinned(v.to_owned())
  Some((project.to_owned(), secret.to_owned()))

Engineering basis: .to_owned() is the conventional &str -> String conversion. .to_string() goes through the Display trait, which for &str is just an indirect way of doing the same thing. Bikeshedding-tier but consistent with the repo's other use of .to_owned (e.g., bitwarden:130 'project_name.to_owned()').
title: v.to_string() instead of v.to_owned() for &str -> String
